Title: The transcription factor CCT30 promotes rice preharvest sprouting by regulating sugar signalling to inhibit the ABA-mediated pathway

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: G46B; ZH11; Nipponbare; K7481 alleles compared
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in seeds at 15 DAF; mainly expressed in embryos; induced after imbibition, peaking at 12 h
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in seeds at 15 DAF; mainly expressed in embryos; induced after imbibition, peaking at 12 h
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Free sugars (glucose, fructose, sucrose, maltose, raffinose) and ABA-related dormancy state
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Freshly harvested seeds, including embryo/endosperm context
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Sugar signalling promotes germination/preharvest sprouting; ABA promotes seed dormancy
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes preharvest sprouting and reduces seed dormancy by enhancing sugar signalling and suppressing the ABA-mediated pathway; increases free sugar accumulation and lowers ABA sensitivity/content
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. qPSR8 was fine-mapped to LOC_Os08g42440/CCT30 and complementation confirmed it as the causal gene. 2. CCT30 knockout enhanced seed dormancy and ABA sensitivity, reduced soluble sugars, and downregulated amylase genes. 3. CCT30 overexpression weakened dormancy, decreased ABA sensitivity/content, and rescued high dormancy in OsbZIP37 knockout background.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL fine mapping; complementation in NIL-1242; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; overexpression; seed germination assays; ABA treatment; ABA quantification by ESI-HPLC-MS/MS; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; GC-MS sugar profiling; glucose treatment assay; Y2H/BiFC/LCI interaction assays
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Freshly harvested seeds at 15 or 30 DAF; imbibition; 5 µM ABA; 1% glucose; 1% sorbitol; 600 µM GA
Common_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with OsbZIP37 in the nucleus
Common_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Sugar signalling, starch hydrolysis-associated sugar release, and ABA-mediated seed dormancy pathway
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter for complementation; overexpression reported but promoter not explicitly stated for provided CCT30-OE lines
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Illumina; accession NA); GC-MS sugar metabolomics; haplotype analysis using RiceVarMap V2.0 public genotype data
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Also previously reported as a heading-date gene; may affect flowering/heading date while improving preharvest sprouting resistance when knocked out
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Good target for breeding rice resistant to preharvest sprouting; loss-of-function alleles can enhance dormancy/PHS resistance, but possible heading-date effects should be considered
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although CCT30 is a transcription factor, under the provided schema it is captured as a core non-enzyme focal gene here because the study emphasizes its interaction-based control of sugar signalling/ABA pathway rather than direct target binding. Overexpression of CCT30 rescues OsbZIP37 knockout-induced dormancy, indicating partially OsbZIP37-independent action or downstream dominance.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Assessed in overexpression and knockout lines; detailed endogenous tissue pattern not reported
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Sugar signalling promotes germination/preharvest sprouting; ABA promotes seed dormancy
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates seed dormancy and promotes germination/preharvest sprouting
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsbZIP37 physically interacts with CCT30 in the nucleus. 2. Overexpression of OsbZIP37 weakens seed dormancy, whereas knockout enhances dormancy. 3. OsbZIP37 negatively affects ABA biosynthesis/signalling gene expression patterns linked to dormancy and positively associates with amylase gene expression.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; seed germination assays; qRT-PCR of ABA pathway and amylase genes; Y2H; BiFC; LCI
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Freshly harvested seeds at 30 DAF grown under common field conditions
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms/interacts in a nuclear complex with CCT30
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Sugar signalling and ABA-mediated regulation of seed dormancy/germination
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter used in modified vector context for combined construct; OsbZIP37-OE overexpression vector 1301U used, exact promoter not explicitly stated in text for single OE construct
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ABA biosynthesis genes NCED1; NCED2; NCED3; NCED4; ABA signalling genes ABI3; ABI5; starch hydrolysis genes Amy1A; Amy1C; Amy3A; possibly coregulated downstream genes OsTPS1; OsDOG1L-3; OsPHY1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Overexpression downregulates NCED1/2/3/4, ABI3, and ABI5 and upregulates Amy1A/Amy3A; knockout shows opposite effects
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation and/or cooperative transcriptional regulation via interaction with CCT30
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conditionally important negative regulator of seed dormancy that works with CCT30 to shift the balance toward sugar signalling and away from ABA-mediated dormancy
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative integration with published OsbZIP37-overexpressing transcriptome data (Miyamoto et al., 2014); qRT-PCR; combined analysis with study RNA-seq data (accession NA)
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: OsbZIP37 downregulation/knockout could be used to enhance dormancy and preharvest sprouting resistance, though interaction with CCT30 and possible broader developmental effects should be considered
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper states OsbZIP37 is a transcriptional activator based on prior work. Direct DNA binding to the highlighted downstream genes was not demonstrated in this study.
